wins: Landed first-wave and second-wave surface-detection slices through real owner repos without widening aoa skills semantics.
Raised an explicit workspace trigger for additive aoa surfaces detect after skills enter and guard when route-drift and owner-layer ambiguity signals appear.
Closed the publish lane honestly through merged PRs, one pinned-ref routing fix, one portable-export mirror sync, and final clean-main workspace sync.
frictions: Generated-output CI truth still depends on workflow-pinned sibling refs rather than whatever local mains happen to contain.
The publish lane crosses approval-sensitive mutation and public-share surfaces.
Owner-family precision beyond current second-wave surfaces still needs more reviewed repetitions before any canon promotion pressure becomes strong.

extracts.title: Cross-Repo Surface Detection Wave Landing Route
extracts.summary: A multi-step scenario for taking surface-detection from seed staging through anchor landing, dependent-repo bridge work, CI-tail repair, merge, and final workspace sync without violating owner boundaries.
notes: The surviving value is scenario discipline across multiple owner repos rather than one executable leaf workflow.

extracts.title: Workspace-Level Surface Trigger Propagation Law
extracts.summary: A reusable actor-law for when workspace sessions should invoke additive surface detection after ingress and mutation gating without confusing router hints for semantic authority.
notes: The surviving object is workspace actor posture about invocation timing and boundary law.

extracts.title: Pinned Validation Matrix Before Generated Publish
extracts.summary: A reusable publish law for rebuilding generated surfaces against workflow-pinned sibling refs before pushing, so local workspace drift does not masquerade as canonical output.
notes: The reusable value is federation publish discipline under CI, not only the routing-tail patch itself.

automation_candidate.title: Cross-Repo Publish And Merge Lane
automation_candidate.current_manual_route: validate -> branch -> commit -> push -> open PR -> wait checks -> inspect CI logs if needed -> patch tail -> merge -> sync local mains

deferrals.title: Automation-ready promotion
deferrals.reason: The lane is real enough to classify, but it still crosses approval-sensitive surfaces and still relies on heavy owner judgment.

deferrals.title: Standalone quest promotion
deferrals.reason: Selection pressure points toward a playbook-owned route, but donor harvest plus checkpoint-aware automation classification still preserve the truth better than a final promotion packet.

deferrals.title: Diagnosis or repair packet
deferrals.reason: The session closed through merge and sync rather than leaving unresolved repair work as the main surviving object.

quest_hooks.reason: The repeated unit is route-shaped and nearer aoa-playbooks than aoa-skills, but a final promotion packet would still be premature.
